title: Caramelized Korean Lettuce Stir-fry
description: A rich, dense, midnight-craving korean dish featuring crispy lettuce and a sweet caramel sauce that is dash-friendly and perfect for any meal.
instructions: Heat vegetable oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add minced garlic and grated ginger to the hot oil and stir-fry for about 1 minute until fragrant.
Remove the garlic and ginger from the pan using a slotted spoon. Add Korean red pepper flakes, soy sauce, sugar, and water. Stir well to combine.
Return the garlic and ginger to the pan and bring the mixture to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for about 2-3 minutes until slightly thickened.
Add the head of lettuce into the pan and stir-fry for another minute or so, making sure to toss gently so as not to break apart the leaves.
Stir in optional vegetable broth if desired, then allow the mixture to steam cook until all the water is absorbed by the leaves, about 5-7 minutes.
Once done, garnish with sliced green onions and serve hot.
